channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Medical and utility tech companies hacked by digital intruders
item.description: Itron and Medtronic reported recent breaches by digital intruders, highlighting vulnerabilities in critical tech sectors. This raises concerns about data security in healthcare and utilities.

item.title: Automate repetitive tasks with Amazon Quick Flows
item.description: Amazon Quick Flows enables users to automate repetitive tasks through AI-powered workflows, enhancing productivity in various applications. This tool simplifies complex processes for businesses.

item.title: Build and deploy an automatic sync solution for Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
item.description: An automated sync solution for Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases was developed to manage S3 events and ingestion jobs efficiently. This serverless architecture ensures knowledge bases remain updated without overloading APIs.

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.description: OpenAI resolved legal issues with Microsoft regarding its $50 billion Amazon deal, allowing product sales on AWS while Microsoft gains from a revenue-sharing agreement. This strengthens OpenAI's market position.

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.description: David Silver's new AI lab, Ineffable Intelligence, raised $1.1 billion to develop AI that learns without human data, achieving a $5.1 billion valuation. This marks a significant investment in innovative AI research.

item.title: South Africa yanks AI policy after AI-assisted drafting invents citations
item.description: South Africa retracted its draft national AI policy after discovering that AI-generated citations were fictional. This incident raises questions about the reliability of AI in policymaking.

item.title: Samsung Wallet just got a travel feature that I hope Google Wallet copies ASAP
item.description: Samsung Wallet introduced a new travel feature, enhancing its functionality for Galaxy users. This addition aims to compete with existing digital wallet solutions.

item.title: Build Strands Agents with SageMaker AI models and MLflow
item.description: A guide on building AI agents using Strands Agents SDK and SageMaker AI models was published, detailing deployment and performance evaluation methods. This resource aids developers in creating advanced AI solutions.

item.title: How Popsa used Amazon Nova to inspire customers with personalised title suggestions
item.description: Popsa utilized Amazon Nova to enhance its Title Suggestion feature, integrating AI for personalized title generation across multiple languages. This innovation improves user engagement and efficiency.

item.title: Friendster rises from the grave to make social media great again
item.description: Friendster has been revived by a new owner aiming to create a unique social media experience without ads or algorithms, focusing on user interaction. This move seeks to attract users disillusioned with current platforms.

item.title: AI reality check: Here's what three companies learned building wallets, homes, and games
item.description: Executives from Citi, Home Depot, and Capcom discuss their experiences with AI agents, highlighting the need for governance and reliability as these tools become integral to business operations.

item.title: The missing step between hype and profit
item.description: The article explores the gap between AI hype and profitability, reflecting on societal concerns and the need for a more grounded approach to AI development.

item.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
item.description: Skye's AI home screen app has garnered investor interest ahead of its launch, indicating a growing demand for AI-integrated mobile applications.

item.title: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description: GitHub's Copilot cloud agent has improved its startup speed by over 20% due to new optimizations, enhancing user experience for developers.

item.title: GitHub Copilot code review will start consuming GitHub Actions minutes on June 1, 2026
item.description: Starting June 1, 2026, GitHub Copilot's code review feature will begin consuming GitHub Actions minutes, impacting how developers manage their resources.

item.title: GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing
item.description: GitHub Copilot will transition to a usage-based billing model starting June 1, requiring users to manage their AI credits for continued access.

item.title: Meta to power its bit barns with energy from space
item.description: Meta is exploring innovative energy solutions, including harnessing solar power from space, to meet the growing demands of its AI operations.

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI's open relationship is now official
item.description: Microsoft and OpenAI have restructured their partnership to allow OpenAI to collaborate with other cloud providers, ending exclusivity while Microsoft retains its license until 2032.

item.title: 6 MacOS settings I immediately change on every new Mac - and why
item.description: The article outlines six essential MacOS settings that enhance user experience, providing practical tips for new Mac users.

item.title: I used a $4 timer to reboot my router, and it actually made my internet faster
item.description: A simple $4 timer was found to effectively reboot a router, resulting in improved internet speed, showcasing an inexpensive solution to common tech issues.

item.title: I tested ChatGPT Images 2.0 vs. Gemini Nano Banana to see which is better - this model wins
item.description: In a comparison of image generation capabilities, ChatGPT Images 2.0 outperformed Gemini Nano Banana across nine tests, showcasing its advanced features.

item.title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
item.description: David Silver, known for AlphaGo, has launched a billion-dollar company focused on creating AI 'superlearners,' aiming to redefine AI development.

item.title: SpaceX dusts off Falcon Heavy for first flight in 18 months
item.description: SpaceX is set to launch its Falcon Heavy rocket for the first time in 18 months, marking a significant return to heavy-lift capabilities.

item.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly developing a phone that would integrate AI agents to replace traditional apps, with mass production anticipated by 2028.

item.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
item.description: Google and Kaggle have launched a new coding course focused on AI agents, aimed at educating developers on this emerging technology.

item.title: This machine is the only way to make nitro cold brew coffee at home - and it's on sale
item.description: The Cumulus Coffee machine, designed for making nitro cold brew coffee at home, is currently on sale with a 20% discount.

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI’s famed AGI agreement is dead
item.description: Microsoft has officially ended its AGI agreement with OpenAI, signaling a shift in their partnership dynamics and future collaboration.

item.title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models
item.description: DeepSeek has unveiled its new flagship AI model, V4, which can handle longer prompts, indicating progress in AI capabilities.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are preparing for a trial that may significantly impact OpenAI's future direction and mission.

item.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
item.description: OpenAI and Microsoft have amended their partnership agreement, simplifying terms and providing clarity for future AI innovations.

item.title: bq-readonly-mcp 0.1.2
item.description: The bq-readonly-mcp 0.1.2 is a read-only BigQuery MCP server designed for various AI clients, featuring auto-LIMIT and cost guard functionalities. This update enhances data management for AI applications.

item.title: sage-ai-cli 1.16.4
item.description: Sage-ai-cli 1.16.4 is a local-first AI coding command-line interface that utilizes free and open models, similar to Claude Code. This version aims to enhance coding workflows for developers.

item.title: ttorch added to PyPI
item.description: ttorch has been added to PyPI as a lightweight C++ tensor and autograd library with Python bindings, facilitating easier integration for machine learning projects. This addition supports developers in building AI applications.

item.title: sage-ai-cli 1.16.3
item.description: Sage-ai-cli 1.16.3 is another update of the local-first AI coding CLI, similar to its previous version. It continues to focus on providing developers with open model capabilities.

item.title: agentweave-ai 0.33.0
item.description: Agentweave-ai 0.33.0 introduces a multi-agent AI collaboration framework that supports various AI models, enhancing cooperative functionalities. This framework aims to improve multi-agent interactions in AI systems.

item.title: sagaflow 0.5.10
item.description: Sagaflow 0.5.10 is a temporal-backed workflow runtime designed for Claude Code skills, allowing for more efficient execution of AI-driven workflows. This update enhances the capabilities of Claude Code.

item.title: Anthropic Targets EU Data Center Market With A Six-Figure Recruitment Drive: Report
item.description: Anthropic is reportedly targeting the EU data center market with a significant recruitment drive, indicating its intent to expand operations in Europe. This move could enhance its competitive position in the AI sector.

item.title: Medline (MDLN) Partners With Symbotic to Implement AI-Powered Warehouse Automation
item.description: Medline has partnered with Symbotic to implement AI-powered warehouse automation, aiming to enhance operational efficiency and inventory management. This partnership highlights the growing trend of AI in logistics.

item.title: GitLab (GTLB), AWS Deepen Partnership to Integrate GitLab Duo With Amazon Bedrock
item.description: GitLab and AWS are deepening their partnership to integrate GitLab Duo with Amazon Bedrock, enhancing development workflows for AI applications. This integration aims to streamline processes for developers.

item.title: The 10 Things You’ll Leave SaaStr AI Annual 2026 Knowing How to Do — That You Don’t Know How to Do Today
item.description: The article discusses the skills B2B founders, CROs, and CMOs will need by 2026 to effectively deploy AI agents, highlighting a widening productivity gap. It emphasizes the urgency for businesses to adapt to AI advancements.

item.title: nnrt added to PyPI
item.description: Neural Network Runtime has been added to PyPI, expanding the availability of tools for developers working with neural networks. This addition is significant for enhancing accessibility to AI development resources.

item.title: callai-agentic-core added to PyPI
item.description: The callai-agentic-core has been introduced to PyPI, offering a lightweight framework for executing and routing tasks for large language model (LLM) agents. This development is important for simplifying the deployment of AI agents.

item.title: subconscious-chat 0.1.7
item.description: Subconscious-chat 0.1.7 has been released, providing a distributed platform for running AI agents across various devices. This platform's significance lies in its versatility and potential for widespread AI applications.

item.title: SAC-pytorch 0.0.26
item.description: SAC-pytorch 0.0.26 has been released, offering an implementation of the Soft Actor Critic algorithm in PyTorch. This update is relevant for researchers and developers working on reinforcement learning.

item.title: Show HN: I made GAI to have LLM agents in Go without heavy frameworks
item.description: A new project called GAI has been showcased, allowing LLM agents to be implemented in Go without heavy frameworks. This innovation is significant for developers looking for lightweight solutions in AI agent deployment.

item.title: SAC-pytorch 0.0.25
item.description: SAC-pytorch 0.0.25 has also been released, further contributing to the Soft Actor Critic implementations in PyTorch. This consistency in updates is vital for maintaining robust tools in the AI community.

item.title: Do AI models ‘understand’ the real world?
item.description: Research indicates that AI models effectively capture human uncertainty, raising questions about their understanding of the real world. This finding is significant for evaluating the capabilities and limitations of AI models.

item.title: Trump turns the WHCD shooting into a pitch for the White House ballroom
item.description: Following an armed incident at the White House Correspondents Dinner, President Trump used the situation to promote his ballroom, showcasing his political strategy. This incident highlights the intersection of politics and media.

item.title: mnemostack 0.2.0b5
item.description: Mnemostack 0.2.0b5 has been released, featuring a memory stack for AI agents that integrates various retrieval and inference technologies. This update is significant for enhancing the efficiency of AI memory management.

item.title: Does reality have a liberal bias?
item.description: A discussion on the political bias of American academics reveals a growing trend towards the Left, supported by data visualizations. This topic is significant for understanding the influence of academia on public discourse.
